qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the status of national resources and services?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find the status of national resources and services on the Status website at https://status.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: How often is the national resources status page updated?
qa_pairs.answer: The national resources status page is updated manually, not automatically.

qa_pairs.question: When do unplanned events appear on the national resources status page?
qa_pairs.answer: Unplanned events will appear on the national resources status page when a staff member has taken note, because the page is updated manually.

qa_pairs.question: Where should I look for the statuses of legacy systems?
qa_pairs.answer: You should consult the appropriate consortium site for statuses of legacy systems.

qa_pairs.question: What is the website for SciNet's legacy system status?
qa_pairs.answer: The website for SciNet's legacy system status is https://docs.scinet.utoronto.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I check the status of SHARCNET legacy systems?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check the status of SHARCNET legacy systems at https://www.sharcnet.ca/my/systems.

qa_pairs.question: What is the link to view the hardware status for the Centre for Advanced Computing?
qa_pairs.answer: The link to view the hardware status for the Centre for Advanced Computing is http://cac.queensu.ca/wiki/index.php/Hardware:Status.

qa_pairs.question: How do I find the cluster status for ACENET?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find the cluster status for ACENET at https://www.ace-net.ca/wiki/Cluster_Status.
